mcq.question: What is data transmission?
mcq.correct: The process of sending data from one device to another
wrong: The process of storing data permanently
The process of deleting data from memory

mcq.question: What is parallel transmission?
mcq.correct: Multiple bits are sent at the same time using multiple wires
wrong: Bits are sent one at a time over one wire
Data flows in one direction only
Packets are sent independently across a network

mcq.question: What is serial transmission?
mcq.correct: Bits are sent one at a time over a single wire
wrong: Multiple bits are sent at once over multiple wires
Data flows both ways at the same time

mcq.question: Which is an advantage of parallel transmission?
mcq.correct: Can be faster over short distances

mcq.question: Which is a disadvantage of parallel transmission?
mcq.correct: Crosstalk

mcq.question: What is skew?
mcq.correct: When bits arrive at different times
wrong: When wires interfere with each other
When data is sent both ways

mcq.question: Which is an advantage of serial transmission?
mcq.correct: Reliable over long distances
wrong: Can only be used inside a computer

mcq.question: Which is a disadvantage of serial transmission?
mcq.correct: Can be slower over very short distances

mcq.question: Which transmission method is commonly used for buses connecting RAM and CPU?
mcq.correct: Parallel

mcq.question: What is simplex transmission?
mcq.correct: Data flows in one direction only
wrong: Data flows both ways at the same time
Multiple bits are sent at once
Data flows both ways but not at the same time

mcq.question: Which is an example of simplex transmission?
mcq.correct: Keyboard sending data to a computer

mcq.question: What is half-duplex transmission?
mcq.correct: Data flows both ways, but not at the same time
wrong: Data flows in one direction only
Data flows both ways at the same time
Bits are sent one at a time

mcq.question: Which is an example of half-duplex transmission?
mcq.correct: Walkie-talkies

mcq.question: What is full-duplex transmission?
mcq.correct: Data flows both ways at the same time
wrong: Data flows in one direction only
Bits are sent one at a time

mcq.question: Which is an example of full-duplex transmission?
mcq.correct: Phone call

mcq.question: What does USB stand for?
mcq.correct: Universal Serial Bus

mcq.question: What type of transmission does USB use?
mcq.correct: Serial

mcq.question: Which is an advantage of USB?
mcq.correct: Can provide power

mcq.question: What does hot-swappable mean?
mcq.correct: The device can be connected or removed without restarting
wrong: The cable gets hot during use
The cable can only be used once

mcq.question: Which is a disadvantage of USB?
mcq.correct: Limited cable length
